summary: Excellent soundboard remaster captures a fire‑hot July ’90 show with standout Dear Mr. Fantasy, Scarlet>Fire and an epic Bird Song jam.
review: The Charlie Miller‑remastered soundboard is crisp, balanced and brings every instrument—especially Brent’s keys and the rhythm section—into clear focus. The performance is widely praised for its energy, highlighted by a smoking second set featuring Dear Mr. Fantasy, Scarlet>Fire, Playing in the Band, Miracle → Crazy Fingers and an extended Bird Song. Overall the crowd and reviewers feel this is one of the strongest shows of the 1990 spring‑summer tour.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive 5‑star comments about both the mix and the musical performance, with only a single moderate 3‑star note; the recording is described as crisp and the show as a tour highlight.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Charlie Miller; reviewers repeatedly note crystal‑clear, even mix with all band members audible (Jerry, Phil, Brent and Bobby), no noticeable dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid but the second set really ignites, delivering long, flowing jams and seamless transitions that keep the momentum high for nearly three hours.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for perfect guitar tone, slide work on Walking Blues and strong backing vocals.
band_member_comments.Phil: Frequently highlighted as clear in the mix, taking lead bass lines in He's Gone and even stepping up to the mic on Just a Little Light.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Brent) is noted as high in the mix, providing soulful keyboards and a powerful post‑He's Gone jam; his final run before leaving the band is felt strongly.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work described as “soulful” and prominently featured throughout, especially on the post‑He's Gone jam and the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart (and Bill Kreutzmann for most of the show) receive praise for driving the grooves, with Mickey’s “Beam” drum solo highlighted.
